note: Fast 404 (project dir fast_404, machine name fast404) is configured entirely through $settings['fast404_*'] keys in settings.php; it has no admin UI, route, config entity, permission, schema, or Drush. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source (Fast404.php, example.settings.fast404.php, fast404.services.yml) to answer. No site mutation.

evals.prompt: Where and how do I configure the Fast 404 module on a Drupal 11 site? Is there an admin settings form?
evals.expected_output: Fast 404 has no admin UI or config form; it is configured entirely via $settings['fast404_*'] keys in settings.php (optionally copied from example.settings.fast404.php into settings.fast404.php and included at the end of settings.php). Enabling the module alone activates the static-file extension check with defaults.

evals.prompt: By default Fast 404 only checks file extensions. How do I make it also serve fast 404s for unknown dynamic Drupal paths?
evals.expected_output: Set $settings['fast404_path_check'] = TRUE in settings.php. This enables Fast404::pathCheck(), which queries the router, path_alias (and optionally redirect) tables and returns a 404 when nothing matches. It is off by default and flagged 'use at your own risk'.

evals.prompt: I want Fast 404 to tell clients a resource is permanently gone rather than just missing. Which setting returns a 410 instead of a 404?
evals.expected_output: Set $settings['fast404_return_gone'] = TRUE. The response() method then returns a 410 Gone instead of 404 Not Found. Default is FALSE.

evals.prompt: How do I make Fast 404 serve my own HTML 404 page, and can it be per-language, in the Fast 404 module?
evals.expected_output: Set $settings['fast404_HTML_error_page'] to a static HTML file path in the docroot (e.g. './my_page.html'), or a langcode-keyed array like ['en'=>'./404-en.html','fr'=>'./404-fr.html'] for per-language pages. Default is FALSE (terse inline HTML).

evals.prompt: The CDN / AdvAgg module generates file URLs that don't exist on disk and Fast 404 is 404ing them. How do I whitelist those URL fragments?
evals.expected_output: Set $settings['fast404_string_whitelisting'] to an array of URL fragments to always allow, e.g. ['cdn/farfuture'] for CDN far-future or ['/advagg_'] for AdvAgg. Any path containing a listed fragment is passed through to Drupal.

evals.prompt: Another module throws a NotFoundHttpException and Drupal's normal 404 page shows instead of the Fast 404 page. How do I make Fast 404 replace it?
evals.expected_output: Set $settings['fast404_not_found_exception'] = TRUE. The event subscriber's onNotFoundException listener (on KernelEvents::EXCEPTION) then replaces the Drupal 404 with the Fast 404 response whenever a NotFoundHttpException is thrown. Default is FALSE.

evals.prompt: Which setting controls the list of file extensions Fast 404 blocks, and what kind of value does it take?
evals.expected_output: $settings['fast404_exts'] holds a regular expression matched against the request path (via preg_match). The default blocks txt, png, gif, jpe?g, css, js, ico, swf, flv, cgi, bat, pl, dll, exe, and asp (excluding /robots and /system/files). Override it with your own regex.

evals.prompt: In the Fast 404 module, which service should I call to get a preconfigured Fast404 object programmatically, and what does it return?
evals.expected_output: Call \Drupal::service('fast404.factory')->createInstance() (Drupal\fast404\Fast404Factory, implementing Fast404FactoryInterface). It returns a Drupal\fast404\Fast404 value object seeded with the current request and langcode, on which you can run extensionCheck(), pathCheck(), isPathBlocked(), and response().
